title: rorippa pyrenaica subsp. hispanica
intro: Rorippa pyrenaica subsp. hispanica, also known as Iberian watercress, is a subspecies of plant in the mustard family, characterized by its small, white flowers and aquatic growth habit. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Iberian watercress include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. It can be prepared as a herbal supplement in the form of capsules or tablets, or as a fresh addition to salads and other culinary dishes.
